title: The Rei Shinji Aquarium Scene
wikitext:
[]: The Rei/Shinji Aquarium Scene [Caution: Spoilers Ahead]             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Fri Aug 21, 2009 5:19 am I've notice this lines while I was watching the camrip w/ subs just now. Aquarium Scene: :I'm glad you were able to make it today, Ayanami. Are you doing alright? :Yes. I've finished my work for the day, so today is a good day. :They're so tiny! They should swim to a larger tank. : Impossible. These small ones can only live in this kind of place. Like me... What in the world would Rei mean when she told Shinji that she's just like the small fishes, which can live in a small tank? Any thoughts people?

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Fri Aug 21, 2009 6:49 am Hmmm... That line (or similar) comes up at least three times in the movie... - Aquarium Scene - Shinji having a flashback in class - Shinji saves Ayanami trapped in Zeruel Alt. translation available here (along with the flashback scene): http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=264756#264756 Zeruel scene in here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=272327#272327

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Fri Aug 21, 2009 7:07 am There is firstly the visual comparison that could be being refered to here. We see Rei floating in a tube later on dont we just before she has Dinner with Gendo. Similarilly the fish are also floating in a tube. Then there is Rei's accertation that she bound to the evangelion and that her life is tied to it possibly which I believe was said during the elevator scene with Asuka*. In that way she is restricted to spending most of her life at NERV and within the confines of an entry tube...hense the comparison with the fish? As LiLi said above aswell, there is also that very symbolic rescue by Shinji from the confines of Zeruel which is again another piece of imagery nodding towards the tube and confinement ideas. * ''I'm merely bound. I'm merely a person that's bound to Eva''.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Fri Aug 21, 2009 10:15 am Maybe she's somehow found knowledge about being a clone and is aware of THE TANK.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Fri Aug 21, 2009 10:43 am Soul Fire wrote: Then there is Rei's accertation that she bound to the evangelion and that her life is tied to it possibly which I believe was said during the elevator scene with Asuka*. * ''I'm merely bound. I'm merely a person that's bound to Eva''.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Aug 21, 2009 11:39 am Isn't the title spoiler-ish?

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Fri Aug 21, 2009 11:43 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: Isn't the title spoiler-ish?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 21, 2009 12:35 pm It's pretty obvious that Rei is bound up and has no freedom whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Fri Aug 21, 2009 12:43 pm After Rei was rescued from Zeruel and befor she was absorbed by Unit 01, it seems that Shinji broke the thing that bounds Rei, but I'm not pretty sure about this one. A 50-50 according to my MAGI computer.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Fri Aug 21, 2009 12:57 pm MugwumpHasNoLiver wrote: Doesn't the aquarium show up in some trailers? Or am I mixing that up with something else? Anyway, Rei probably feels that, like the fish, she has nowhere else to go. Just as the fish have been in that tiny tube their entire life, Rei knows nothing outside of her tiny tube, the Eva and Nerve. Who's getting either of them a bigger tank, huh? NOBODY. They're trapped and alone and they're going to die mierable, probably painful deaths, stewing in their own obscurity. Rei knows this, and is appropriately miserable.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Aug 21, 2009 3:52 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Isn't the title spoiler-ish?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Aug 21, 2009 3:55 pm NAveryW wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: Isn't the title spoiler-ish?

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Fri Aug 21, 2009 7:10 pm for an alternate translation* that, IMO, gives a different meaning to her words...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 21, 2009 8:48 pm AuraTwilight wrote: It's pretty obvious that Rei is bound up and has no freedom whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sat Aug 22, 2009 3:38 am The question is this: What bounds Rei? What wold be the things that makes her compare herself to the little fishes?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Aug 22, 2009 3:59 am From 1.0: Rei: It's a bond, with everyone. I have nothing else. At that point in 2.0, all she has is Eva, Gendo, and her responsibilities to them.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sat Aug 22, 2009 4:10 am But then comes Shinji which reveals things to her which is called emotions. She knows how it feels but she can't express it well, all thanks to that asshole Gendou who treats Rei as his personal doll, in which he manipulates her to his likings.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sat Aug 22, 2009 4:16 am ^ I have been wondering, why does he keep her in that ruined building? To keep her isolated? But then, why bother sending her to school?

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Sat Aug 22, 2009 6:00 am Its never been said explcitly that he keeps her there. It suits her character ...well it did, to be alone and away from other people I suppose.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sat Aug 22, 2009 6:04 am Soul Fire wrote: Its never been said explcitly that he keeps her there. It suits her character ...well it did, to be alone and away from other people I suppose.
